mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 129527k in Jan 2010 reflects ongoing post-crisis employment weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where PAYEMS declines month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate near zero (FEDFUNDS = 0.11%) combined with sustained low rates over 6+ months signals an extended monetary accommodation regime that reduces equity risk premiums and supports forward equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 6+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.11% in Jan 2010 reflects zero lower bound policy
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 1.5% or t-stat < 1.5) during periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% for 6+ months, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with increasing housing starts (HOUST) signals strengthening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, which drives positive earnings revisions and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 355777M and HOUST at 557k in Dec 2009 show early recovery signals
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher (mean return < 1.0% or t-stat < 1.2) when both RSAFS and HOUST rise for 2+ consecutive months, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 0.5% month-over-month signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, which supports corporate profitability and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 100.3043 in Dec 2009 shows recovery from crisis lows
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.3% or t-stat < 1.0) in months when INDPRO rises > 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 9.5% signals persistent labor market slack that constrains wage growth and consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.7% in Jan 2010 reflects elevated post-crisis unemployment
regime_hint: post-crisis labor market weakness with high slack
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean return > 0.5% or t-stat < -1.2) when UNRATE > 9.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with accommodative monetary policy (FEDFUNDS near zero) creates a regime where real rates are deeply negative, compressing bond yields and driving equity valuations higher through duration effects, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.541 and FEDFUNDS at 0.11% in early 2010 show negative real rate regime
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.8% or t-stat < 1.1) when both conditions are met, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration signals strengthening economic fundamentals that support equity earnings growth. When quarterly GDP growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days are positive as investors reprice growth expectations upward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 2.5% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13155.0B in Q4 2009 reflects early recovery from recession
regime_hint: early-cycle expansion with positive growth acceleration
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 1.5% or t-stat < 1.3) when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Deteriorating labor market conditions (PAYEMS declining and UNRATE rising simultaneously) signal a regime where corporate earnings face downward pressure from both reduced consumer demand and rising unemployment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month while UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market weakness persists in early 2010 with PAYEMS at 129527k and UNRATE at 9.7%
regime_hint: labor market deterioration in post-crisis recovery
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean return > 0.5% or t-stat < -1.2) when both PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
